Module com.cuatrifasico.battleshi
Package com.cuatrifasico.battleshi.controller

Class GameController

java.lang.Object
com.cuatrifasico.battleshi.controller.GameController
public class GameController extends Object
Controller for game-view.fxml. Wires the procedurally-built board and fleet tray views into their FXML placeholders, drives the ship-placement phase through PlacementController, and then takes over for the combat phase: firing shots on the opponent board, running the machine's turns (via MachineTurnThread / FleetPlacementThread), painting result markers, detecting victory/defeat, autosaving progress, and wiring the four action buttons plus the win/lose overlay.

    private final class 
    GameController.ShotClickAdapter
    Reacts to a click on the opponent board: fires the human shot there

    Saves progress (if a match is in progress) and returns to the main menu.

    handleHumanShot(Coordinate coordinate)
    Validates and applies a human shot at coordinate, under the same synchronized (gameSession) monitor that MachineTurnThread uses, so a click that sneaks in while the machine thread is about to wake up can never race it.

    handleRestart()
    During placement, restart just resets ship placement as before.
    private void
    hideInstructionsOverlay()
     
    private void
    initialize()
    Called automatically by the FXML loader once every @FXML field has been injected.

    Initializes the tutorial carousel.

    loadInstructionPages()
     
    private void
    markShipSunk(Shot shot)
     
    private void
    onMachineFleetReady(boolean success)
    Callback for FleetPlacementThread: once the machine's fleet is placed, creates the GameSession (human goes first), wires the opponent board for shots, enables the debug fleet-preview button, and performs the very first autosave.
    private void
    onMachineShot(Coordinate coordinate, CellState result, boolean gameOver)
    Callback for MachineTurnThread, always invoked on the FX Application Thread.
    private void
    persistProgress()
    Auto saves after every move (human or machine), per the project's HU-5: while the match is GameState.IN_PROGRESS the session is written to disk; once it ends, the save is deleted so "Continue Game" is disabled on the next launch.
    private void
    processTurnQueue()
    Drains turnQueue, painting a marker for every buffered shot, then auto saves, refreshes the turn indicators, shows the result overlay if the match just ended, or spawns the next MachineTurnThread if it is now the machine's turn.

    recolorShip(javafx.scene.Group shipNode, String newFillClass)

    Loads a brand-new game-view.fxml, reusing the current nickname.

    renderShipSilhouette(BoardGridView view, Ship ship, String fillStyleClass)
    Draws a ship's visual body on the given board view, using the same placement-origin math PlacementController uses for live placement.
    private void
    renderShotMarker(Shot shot)
    Paints the appropriate MarkerShapeFactory marker for a shot on whichever board it landed on: the opponent's board for a human shot, the player's own board for a machine shot.

    Entry point for resuming a previously saved match.

    private void

    Toggles the debug-only preview of the machine's full fleet, showing/hiding translucent ship silhouettes on the opponent board without affecting game rules.
    private void
    updateTurnIndicators()
    Reflects GameSession.isHumanTurn() on both boards' turn-indicator borders.

    • turnQueue

      private final ArrayDeque<Shot> turnQueue
      Buffers the model-level outcome of a shot (human or machine) between the moment it is applied to the Board/GameSession under lock, and the moment its marker is actually painted on screen. Using an ArrayDeque here — distinct from the one RandomHuntStrategy keeps internally for its own targeting candidates — is what satisfies the rubric's point 5 (a dedicated non-array structure for turn/shot processing): every shot, from either side, is enqueued here and drained by processTurnQueue() in strict order, which keeps marker rendering, persistence, and turn hand-off from ever interleaving even if several results arrive back to back.

    • enemyFleetPreviewNodes

      private final List<javafx.scene.Node> enemyFleetPreviewNodes
      Debug-only ship previews currently shown for viewEnemyButton.

    • enemyFleetPreviewVisible

      private boolean enemyFleetPreviewVisible

    • instructionPages

      private final List<javafx.scene.image.Image> instructionPages
      Tutorial pages shown in the instructions overlay. Only one ImageView is reused for every page.

    • currentInstructionPage

      private int currentInstructionPage
      Zero-based index of the currently visible tutorial page.

  • Constructor Details

    • GameController

      public GameController()

  • Method Details

    • initialize

      private void initialize()
      Called automatically by the FXML loader once every @FXML field has been injected.

    • setNickname

      public void setNickname(String nickname)
      Sets the nickname shown above the player's board. Called by MenuController right after loading this view.
      Parameters:
      nickname - The nickname to display.

    • resumeFromSession

      public void resumeFromSession(GameSession session)
      Entry point for resuming a previously saved match. Restores the human's already-placed fleet, replays the full shot history onto both boards, wires the opponent board for further shots, and either waits for the human's input or immediately kicks off a machine turn — whichever the saved session says is next.
      Parameters:
      session - The session loaded from disk.

    • startCombat

      private void startCombat()
      Called when the player presses "Play" once their fleet is complete. Freezes the placement board, wraps the placed fleet into a HumanPlayer, and places the machine's fleet on a background thread so the UI never blocks.

    • onMachineFleetReady

      private void onMachineFleetReady(boolean success)
      Callback for FleetPlacementThread: once the machine's fleet is placed, creates the GameSession (human goes first), wires the opponent board for shots, enables the debug fleet-preview button, and performs the very first autosave.
      Parameters:
      success - true if the machine's fleet was placed correctly.

    • handleHumanShot

      private void handleHumanShot(Coordinate coordinate)
      Validates and applies a human shot at coordinate, under the same synchronized (gameSession) monitor that MachineTurnThread uses, so a click that sneaks in while the machine thread is about to wake up can never race it.
      Parameters:
      coordinate - The targeted cell on the machine's board.

    • onMachineShot

      private void onMachineShot(Coordinate coordinate, CellState result, boolean gameOver)
      Callback for MachineTurnThread, always invoked on the FX Application Thread. The session itself was already updated (shot recorded, turn/state transitioned) inside the thread's own locked section, so this only needs to enqueue the result for rendering and let processTurnQueue() take it from there.
      Parameters:
      coordinate - The cell the machine fired at.
      result - The resulting cell state.
      gameOver - true if this shot destroyed the human fleet.

    • processTurnQueue

      private void processTurnQueue()
      Drains turnQueue, painting a marker for every buffered shot, then auto saves, refreshes the turn indicators, shows the result overlay if the match just ended, or spawns the next MachineTurnThread if it is now the machine's turn.

    • renderShotMarker

      private void renderShotMarker(Shot shot)
      Paints the appropriate MarkerShapeFactory marker for a shot on whichever board it landed on: the opponent's board for a human shot, the player's own board for a machine shot.
      Parameters:
      shot - The shot to render.

    • markShipSunk

      private void markShipSunk(Shot shot)

    • recolorShip

      private void recolorShip(javafx.scene.Group shipNode, String newFillClass)

    • updateTurnIndicators

      private void updateTurnIndicators()
      Reflects GameSession.isHumanTurn() on both boards' turn-indicator borders.

    • showResultOverlay

      private void showResultOverlay(GameState state)
      Shows the win/lose overlay and clears both turn indicators.
      Parameters:
      state - Either GameState.PLAYER_WON or GameState.MACHINE_WON.

    • persistProgress

      private void persistProgress()
      Auto saves after every move (human or machine), per the project's HU-5: while the match is GameState.IN_PROGRESS the session is written to disk; once it ends, the save is deleted so "Continue Game" is disabled on the next launch. A no-op before combat starts (no session yet).

    • handleRestart

      private void handleRestart()
      During placement, restart just resets ship placement as before. During combat, restart discards the current match entirely and starts a brand-new one (fresh placement phase).

      Team note: this combat-phase semantic was left ambiguous in the project brief — confirm with the team/professor whether "restart" should instead be disabled once shots have been fired.

    • handleExit

      private void handleExit()
      Saves progress (if a match is in progress) and returns to the main menu.

    • goToMenu

      private void goToMenu()

    • reloadFreshGame

      private void reloadFreshGame()
      Loads a brand-new game-view.fxml, reusing the current nickname.

    • toggleEnemyFleetPreview

      private void toggleEnemyFleetPreview()
      Toggles the debug-only preview of the machine's full fleet, showing/hiding translucent ship silhouettes on the opponent board without affecting game rules. Disabled until the machine's fleet actually exists.

    • loadInstructionPages

      private void loadInstructionPages()

    • initializeInstructionsCarousel

      private void initializeInstructionsCarousel()
      Initializes the tutorial carousel.

      The instructions overlay uses a single ImageView that is updated when the player changes pages. The content is clipped using a rounded rectangle so the images perfectly match the overlay card while keeping the border visible.

    • showPreviousInstruction

      private void showPreviousInstruction()
      Displays the previous tutorial page.

      The carousel wraps to the last page when the first page is reached.

    • showNextInstruction

      private void showNextInstruction()
      Displays the next tutorial page.

      The carousel wraps back to the first page after the last one.

    • hideInstructionsOverlay

      private void hideInstructionsOverlay()

    • renderShipSilhouette

      private javafx.scene.Node renderShipSilhouette(BoardGridView view, Ship ship, String fillStyleClass)
      Draws a ship's visual body on the given board view, using the same placement-origin math PlacementController uses for live placement. Reused for repainting an already-placed human fleet on resume, and for the enemy fleet debug preview.
      Parameters:
      view - The board view to draw on.
      ship - The ship to render.
      fillStyleClass - The fill style class (normal body vs. shadow preview).
      Returns:
      The created node, so callers can track and remove it later.
